attempt: Carry Ticket 36's lesson across the problem: a proof route that only controls density, mass, or bounded statistics must be upgraded to a pointwise theorem, because a sparse exceptional object can still falsify the conjecture.

bounded_result.stress_result: An infinite zero-density set of even exceptions would pass almost-all and density tests while falsifying Goldbach.
bounded_result.discarded_route: almost-all Goldbach positivity used as pointwise Goldbach proof
bounded_result.retained_route: explicit pointwise lower bound for every even N beyond a verified cutoff

obstruction: Sparse or null exceptional sets are logically invisible to many aggregate metrics. A complete proof must eliminate every exceptional object, not merely show that exceptions are rare.
candidate_theorem: There exists an explicit N0 and positive lower bound G(N)>0 for every even N>N0, with all small N checked independently.
next_experiment: Stress singular-series and minor-arc margins against adversarial sparse exceptional even integers.
claim_boundary: No goldbach proof and no certified goldbach counterexample.
